pageTitle.value: Lungwevungu River Expedition Episode 2 - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: The Zambezi River, home to one of the natural wonders of the world, Victoria Falls, is a life source for millions of people, across six countries. National Geographic Explorer Steve Boyes and his team have travelled over 900 kilometres from the remote Angolan highlands to the Zambian border to better understand the health of this incredible waterway. This is the first of the Great Spine of Africa expeditions that will establish ecological baselines to reveal how Africa’s great rivers are changing.

WaPageTitle.value: The Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Lungwevungu River - Episode 2

metadescription.value: Conservationist Steve Boyes is collecting extensive data of the Lungwevungu River to create baselines for future study. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: The Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Lungwevungu River - Episode 2

description.value: Recording a river for the future

title.value: The Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Lungwevungu River - Episode 2

text.value: The Zambezi River, home to one of the natural wonders of the world, Victoria Falls, is a life source for millions of people, across six countries. National Geographic Explorer Steve Boyes and his team have travelled over 900 kilometres from the remote Angolan highlands to the Zambian border to better understand the health of this incredible waterway. This is the first of the Great Spine of Africa expeditions that will establish ecological baselines to reveal how Africa’s great rivers are changing.

pageCaption.value: Vreni Häusserman is a German biologist who is exploring the unique marine life of the Patagonian fjords in the south of Chile. These extraordinary and delicate underwater environments are suffering from the effects of intensive fish farming and global warming. Vreni is planning ever-deeper dives to study, understand and preserve this unique biodiversity before it is too late.

metadescription.value: Vreni Häusserman is a German biologist who is exploring the unique marine life of the Patagonian fjords in the south of Chile. These extraordinary and delicate underwater environments are suffering from the effects of intensive fish farming and global warming. Vreni is planning ever-deeper dives to study, understand and preserve this unique biodiversity before it is too late.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 1

pageCaption.value: “Think like an ocean”. Working to protect the iconic Galápagos Islands Marine Reserve, Sylvia Earle and her Mission Blue team led a groundbreaking expedition to assess the health of the ocean after nearly 25 years of protection. What they found could be used as a blueprint of success for the rest of the planet.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 1

metadescription.value: 25 years after the creation of the Galápagos Marine Reserve, an expedition has been launched to collect new data. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 2

pageCaption.value: Mission Blue has been helping to track animals in the Galápagos Marine Reserve to analyse their well-being around the archipelago. Through this work, legendary oceanographer Sylvia Earle and a team of scientists are making incredible insights into the connectivity of the Galápagos with the rest of the planet – including discovering DNA sequences that could be completely new to science.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 2

metadescription.value: Using eDNA, researchers are aiming to characterize the marine diversity of the Galápagos to protect specific habitats. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Lungwevungu River Expedition Episode 3 - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: Travelling the equivalent of a round-the-world trip, explorer Steve Boyes and his team are undertaking a series of pioneering expeditions to find new species and establish ecological baselines of Africa’s greatest rivers. Their first journey took them 900 kilometres from the remote Angolan highlands to the border with Zambia following a tributary to the mighty Zambezi River. What they discover will help build ecological resilience and protect waters that millions of people and countless unique ecosystems depend on.

WaPageTitle.value: The Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Lungwevungu River - Episode 3

metadescription.value: Conservationist Steve Boyes is encouraging local scientific teams to help in his effort to survey Africa’s major rivers. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: The Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Lungwevungu River - Episode 3

pageTitle.value: Lungwevungu River Expedition Episode 1 - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: Venturing into the remote Angolan wilderness, conservationist and National Geographic Fellow Steve Boyes led an expedition to increase scientific knowledge of the mighty Zambezi River, on which millions of people depend. The discoveries they have made will help find ways to protect these vital waterways as the climate changes.

WaPageTitle.value: The Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Lungwevungu River - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Conservationist Steve Boyes and his team have set out to scientifically document the unexplored Lungwevungu River. Watch the video on rolex.org.
